1980s.title: Garment exports enter the price story
1980s.body: Bangladesh's ready-made garment sector expanded from a small base while food prices, import bills, and monetary management shaped household purchasing power.
1980s.event: RMG export takeoff and late-military-era policy normalization
1980s.driver: Food prices, import costs, money supply
1980s.outcome: The index begins after the early post-independence inflation era, so the route starts in 1986.
1980s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

1990s.title: Democratic transition and export acceleration
1990s.body: After the 1990 democratic transition, apparel exports, remittances, and donor-supported reforms became larger forces in the economy while prices still moved with food supply shocks.
1990s.event: Return to parliamentary democracy and export-led growth
1990s.driver: Food harvests, credit conditions, imported inputs
1990s.outcome: Purchasing power changed more gradually than in crisis decades, but inflation still mattered for wage earners.

2000s.title: Food and fuel shocks hit household budgets
2000s.body: The 2007-2008 global food and fuel price shock pushed basic costs higher, making rice, energy, and transport central to the purchasing power experience.
2000s.event: Global food-price spike and caretaker-government period
2000s.driver: Rice prices, fuel costs, global commodity pressure
2000s.outcome: The price index moved faster as essentials became more expensive relative to wages.
2000s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

2010s.title: Fast growth, wages, and urban costs
2010s.body: Industrial growth, garment wages, remittances, and infrastructure spending changed the economy, while rent, food, and transport costs kept pressure on urban households.
2010s.event: Middle-income growth push and garment-sector wage debates
2010s.outcome: Inflation was less dramatic than in the 2020s, but compounding still reduced the value of older taka amounts.

2020s.title: Pandemic, exchange pressure, and energy costs
2020s.body: COVID-19 disruption, global commodity prices, energy adjustments, and pressure on foreign reserves made the early 2020s a sharp purchasing-power moment.
2020s.outcome: The 2024 endpoint captures a much higher price level than the 1986 starting point.
